campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed an upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategy
Public hearing on government code section 3502
Boogie Festival event this weekend
Housing security concerns

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved preliminary steps for what appears to be a major infrastructure or development project, including an engineer's report and a formal notice of intent. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city (giving it more local control) and reviewed potential new tax options to fund city services.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report approval for fiscal year project
Potential charter city status change and local control questions
New tax options and revenue estimates being considered
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption on May 20, 2026

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, confirm a new youth commissioner, and set up the November 2026 municipal election. The council also approved several agreements and permits related to development projects, including a new residential tract map and software/service contracts to support town operations.
keyTopics: November 3, 2026 general municipal election scheduled
New youth commissioner appointment ratified
Tract 10675 development project final map approved
Multi-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. authorized
Building division plan check services contracts approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 3, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for June 9 and June 16, 2026. They reviewed which items to add, decided on timing for different agenda items, and approved retroactive free use of the Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for an event on June 7.
keyTopics: Finalized June 9, 2026 council agenda items and scheduling
Reviewed draft agenda for June 16, 2026 meeting
Approved retroactive free use of Janet Gray Hayes Rotunda for June 7 event

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026 with both in-person and online participation available. Residents could attend virtually via Zoom or phone and submit public comments during designated agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom (ID: 814 1954 3379) and phone (669-900-9128)
Public comment procedures using Zoom 'raise hand' feature or phone *9 key
Phone participants can unmute with *6 to speak during their turn

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 2, 2026. The first was a closed-door session (details not public), followed by a second special meeting with items handled as special orders of business.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held
Special order items on council agenda

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 2, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to officially recognize June as LGBTQ+ Pride Month and to acknowledge the United Nations World Environment Day on June 5th. The meeting also included a public comment period where residents could speak about any city matters not listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation
UN World Environment Day recognition
Public comment period for non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 3, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding Charter Review Committee meetings where residents can participate in person or online via Zoom. The city is accepting written public comments from residents who email the city attorney before 10 a.m. the day before each meeting.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings happening in-person at City Hall
Remote participation available via Zoom for residents who can't attend in person
Written public comments accepted via email to cityattorney@santaclaraca.gov
Deadline for comment submissions is 10 a.m. one day before each meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 2, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's spending plan for fiscal year 2026-27 and handle routine administrative matters like approving past meeting minutes and setting the calendar for upcoming meetings. The council also discussed changes to how city commissions schedule their appointment cycles.
keyTopics: Approved the 2026-27 operating budget for city spending
Updated the commission appointment process to quarterly cycles
Reviewed upcoming agenda items and council member requests

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 27, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed its upcoming 5-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) and discussed applying for a state prohousing designation program that could help increase housing development. The council also received updates on modernizing downtown parking systems, though that item wasn't fully discussed at this meeting.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan and budget priorities
Prohousing Designation Program application to support housing development
Downtown parking modernization initiatives
Finance Committee recommendations on capital spending
